summary: draft --from-next-candidate now turns completed-goal candidates into reviewable draft AC with concrete measurement and threshold text. The generated opennori-adoption-dogfood draft keeps ACCEPTANCE-BASIS pending approval while telling the user how to judge non-OpenNori adoption friction, and tests prevent the old generic candidate-direction template from returning.

sources.label: npx vitest run test/core.test.js -t 'evidence can drive the workflow'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/core.test.js -t 'evidence can drive the workflow'

sources.label: npx vitest run test/cli-commands.test.js -t 'draft command module creates draft contracts from completed goal candidates'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/cli-commands.test.js -t 'draft command module creates draft contracts from completed goal candidates'

reviewability: Rerun the two targeted tests and the draft command, then inspect the generated draft criteria for concrete user action, report/evidence review, friction judgment, and ACCEPTANCE-BASIS pending approval.
limitations: This proves candidate-to-draft quality for built-in next-loop candidates; the new dogfood draft is still unapproved and does not prove external project adoption by itself.
